disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Focal, acutely tender lump at the upper eyelid margin over ~24 hours with a visible yellow pustule at the base of an eyelash follicle
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe the lump — where exactly is it and what does it look like?
Is there a whitehead or a yellow spot on it, and where on the eyelid is it?
Tell me more about the swelling on your eyelid.
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's right on the edge of my upper eyelid, and there's a little yellow head on it, sort of like a whitehead, at the base of one of my eyelashes. It's really tender.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Foreign-body sensation and reactive watering of the affected eye, secondary to mechanical lid irritation
trigger_question_examples: Does it feel like anything is in your eye, and is your eye watering?
Are there any other eye symptoms, like grittiness or tearing?
How does the eye itself feel, apart from the lump?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, it feels like there's a bit of grit or an eyelash stuck in there, and the eye keeps watering because of it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Visual acuity is subjectively normal — no blurring or loss of vision in the affected eye (pertinent negative screening for orbital involvement)
trigger_question_examples: Has your vision changed at all — any blurring or loss of sight in that eye?
Can you see normally out of the affected eye?
Is your eyesight in that eye any different from usual?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, my vision's completely normal — I can see fine out of it, no blurring or anything.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I haven't really thought about my actual eyesight, just the lump.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No double vision and no pain on eye movement (pertinent negative screening for orbital cellulitis)
trigger_question_examples: Does it hurt when you move your eye, and are you seeing double at all?
Any pain looking around, or double vision?
When you move your eye up, down or sideways, is it painful?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, moving my eye doesn't hurt and I'm not seeing double. It's just the lump on the lid that's sore.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I've been keeping the eye shut so I haven't moved it much.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Redness and swelling are confined to the lid margin — not diffusely involving the whole eyelid, periorbital tissue or cheek — and the patient is afebrile (pertinent negatives screening for preseptal/orbital cellulitis and systemic spread)
trigger_question_examples: Has the redness spread across your whole eyelid or onto your cheek, or is it just on the lid edge?
Do you have a fever or feel generally unwell?
Is the swelling spreading, and how are you feeling in yourself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The redness is only right on the edge of the lid — it hasn't spread over my whole eyelid or onto my cheek. And no, I don't have a fever, I feel okay in myself otherwise.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: It's hard to tell exactly how far it goes.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: History of chronic meibomian gland dysfunction and recent vigorous eye rubbing exacerbating the inflammation
trigger_question_examples: Have you had anything like this before, or any ongoing eyelid problems?
Have you been rubbing or touching the eye?
Do you get recurrent blocked glands or gritty eyelids?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I do get blocked, gritty eyelids now and then. And yeah, I've been rubbing it a lot because it feels like there's something in there.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient lives alone and is highly distressed with fear of ocular damage — psychosocial context relevant to safety-net communication
trigger_question_examples: How are you coping with this — is there anyone at home with you?
You sound worried; can you tell me what's frightening you about it?
Do you have someone who can help keep an eye on things at home?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I live on my own, and honestly I'm pretty scared — I keep worrying I'm going to damage my eye or lose my sight.
